sections.paragraph:
νηκτός, ή, όν (νήχω; Plut., Mor. 636e; 776c; Vett. Val. 344, 15; Philo, Op. M. 63; Jos., Ant. 8, 44) pert. to the act of swimming, swimming, subst. τὸ νηκτόν that which swims (Ps.-Aristot., De Mundo 398b, 31; Galen XVIII 1 p. 207 K.) τῆς θαλάσσης τὰ νηκτά what swims in the sea PtK 2 p. 14, 18.—DELG s.v. 1 νέω.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Ps.-Aristot Ps.-Aristot , s. Aristot.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 671
